rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical halo-heterocyclization of N-allylbenzamides using 1,2-dichloroethane (DCE) as a chloride shuttle to construct 5-halomethyl-2-oxazolines, 2-thiazolines, 1,3-oxazines and isoxazolines
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of DCE generates a chloroethyl radical/carbanion that fragments to ethylene and Cl-; the released Cl- is then anodically oxidized to electrophilic Cl+ which initiates chloro-cyclization of the N-allylbenzamide (DCE acts as an e-shuttle halogenating reagent in a paired electrolysis).

key_intermediates.name: chloroethyl radical from one-electron reduction of DCE (A)
electrophilic Cl+ from anodic oxidation of Cl-
chloriranium/chloronium complex C of N-allylbenzamide with Cl+

electron_flow: At the cathode, DCE is reduced (2 e-) to a chloroethyl radical (A) then carbanion (B), which beta-eliminates Cl- and releases ethylene. The liberated Cl- migrates to the anode where it is oxidized to electrophilic Cl+; this Cl+ activates the alkene of the N-allylbenzamide (intermediate C), the amide oxygen cyclizes onto the chloronium (intermediate D), and loss of H+ delivers the 5-chloromethyl-2-oxazoline product 2aa.

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1aa, DCE, and Et4NBF4 in MeCN
probe_reagents.observation: No oxidation wave for 1aa in 0-2.0 V, ruling out direct anodic oxidation of the alkene to a radical cation; DCE shows a reduction peak at 0.95 V and an oxidation peak at 1.41 V (Cl- generated from DCE reduction is oxidized at the anode).

notes: An alternative mechanism via direct anodic oxidation of the alkene 1aa to a radical cation was explicitly excluded by CV (no oxidation of 1aa in 0-2.0 V). The mechanism is supported mainly by CV and analogy to prior work; no radical traps, KIE, or DFT calculations are reported on these pages.
